summary: Frontend translation testing completed. Dashboard, Settings, Reports, and Orders pages are fully translated to English. However, HR, Inventory, and Delivery pages have significant translation gaps with many Arabic texts remaining.

Inventory Page: Add translations for stats cards: قيمة المخزون->Stock Value, نقص المخزون->Low Stock, إجمالي الأصناف->Total Items
Inventory Page: Add translations for tabs: المواد الخام->Raw Materials, المنتجات النهائية->Finished Products
Inventory Page: Add translation for empty state: لا توجد أصناف->No items
Delivery Page: Add translation for page header: إدارة التوصيل->Delivery Management

Delivery Page: Add translations for tabs: السائقين والحسابات->Drivers & Accounts, الخريطة->Map, طلبات جاهزة للتوصيل->Ready for Delivery

Delivery Page: Add translations for driver card: متاح->Available, غير مدفوع->Unpaid, مدفوع اليوم->Paid Today, عرض التفاصيل->View Details, تعديل->Edit
critical_code_review_comments: The useTranslation hook and autoTranslate.js dictionary are working correctly for pages that use them
HR.js, Inventory.js, and Delivery.js pages have hardcoded Arabic strings that are not wrapped with the t() translation function
The translation dictionary (autoTranslate.js) already contains most of the required translations, but the page components are not using them
Need to wrap all Arabic text strings in these pages with the t() function from useTranslation hook
context_for_next_testing_agent: Translation testing revealed that Dashboard, Settings, Reports, and Orders pages are fully translated to English. However, HR, Inventory, and Delivery pages have significant translation gaps. The translation dictionary (autoTranslate.js) contains the required translations, but these pages have hardcoded Arabic strings not wrapped with the t() function. Main agent needs to update HR.js, Inventory.js, and Delivery.js to use the useTranslation hook for all text strings.
